# Tide Prediction

## Why the predicted speed varies

Predicting the tides means tracking the elliptical orbit of the Moon around the Earth. Because the Moon's distance varies along that elliptical orbit, the strength of its gravitational pull on the ocean rises and falls, so the predicted rate of change accelerates and then decelerates over the course of the month. A good forecast has to model how the Moon's varying speed shifts the timing of every high and low water.

## Mechanical tide engines

Before electronic computers, these lunar and solar cycles were modeled by elaborate tide-predicting engines. Brass and bronze machines summed dozens of harmonic constituents through interlocking wheels and swept pointers across graduated dials to show the height of the water on any chosen date.
